audit_items.proof_trace: At line 71, `ber_strdup(urlstr)` can return NULL on OOM; there is no NULL check before line 80 unconditionally passes `p` to `ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(p)`, which immediately dereferences `*s` (url.c:1625), causing a NULL pointer dereference crash. On non-Unix platforms (`LDAP_DIRSEP != '/'`), `strchr(NULL, '/')` at line 76 is additionally triggered. The proof fails: the function has no guard protecting any use of `p` after allocation.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker who can trigger a low-memory condition (e.g., via concurrent requests exhausting heap) while a `file:` URL is processed through `ldif_fetch_url()` will cause `ber_strdup()` to return NULL; the subsequent unconditional `ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(NULL)` dereferences the NULL pointer, crashing the LDAP server process (DoS via SIGSEGV).
